children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: There shall be established within the Administration a National Mental Health and Substance Use Policy Laboratory (referred to in this section as the “Laboratory”).
continue to carry out the authorities and activities that were in effect for the Office of Policy, Planning, and Innovation as such Office existed prior to ;December 13, 2016

identify, coordinate, and facilitate the implementation of policy changes likely to have a significant effect on mental health, mental illness, recovery supports, and the prevention and treatment of substance use disorder services;

work with the Center for Behavioral Health Statistics and Quality to collect, as appropriate, information from grantees under programs operated by the Administration in order to evaluate and disseminate information on evidence-based practices, including culturally and linguistically appropriate services, as appropriate, and service delivery models;
provide leadership in identifying and coordinating policies and programs, including evidence-based programs, related to mental and substance use disorders;

children.label: A
children.text: identify any such programs or activities that are duplicative;

children.label: B
children.text: identify any such programs or activities that are not evidence-based, effective, or efficient; and

children.label: C
children.text: formulate recommendations for coordinating, eliminating, or improving programs or activities identified under subparagraph (A) or (B) and merging such programs or activities into other successful programs or activities;

children.label: A
children.text: encourage the implementation and replication of evidence-based practices; and

children.label: B
children.text: provide technical assistance to applicants for funding, including with respect to justifications for such programs and activities; and
carry out other activities as deemed necessary to continue to encourage innovation and disseminate evidence-based programs and practices.

children.heading: Evidence-based practices and service delivery models
children.label: c Evidence-based practices and service delivery models
i
children.text: the coordination between mental health and physical health providers;

children.label: ii
children.text: the coordination among such providers and the justice and corrections system; and

children.label: iii
children.text: the cost effectiveness, quality, effectiveness, and efficiency of health care services furnished to adults with a serious mental illness, children with a serious emotional disturbance, or individuals in a mental health crisis; and

children.label: B
children.text: may include clinical protocols and practices that address the needs of individuals with early serious mental illness.

children.label: A
children.text: the Chief Medical Officer appointed under ;section 290aa(g) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: representatives of the National Institute of Mental Health, the National Institute on Drug Abuse, and the National Institute on Alcohol Abuse and Alcoholism, on an ongoing basis;

children.label: C
children.text: other appropriate Federal agencies;

children.label: D
children.text: clinical and analytical experts with expertise in psychiatric medical care and clinical psychological care, health care management, education, corrections health care, and mental health court systems, as appropriate; and

children.label: E
children.text: other individuals and agencies as determined appropriate by the Assistant Secretary.

children.heading: Deadline for beginning implementation
children.label: d Deadline for beginning implementation
children.text: The Laboratory shall begin implementation of this section not later than .January 1, 2018

children.label: i
children.text: enhancing the prevention, diagnosis, intervention, and treatment of, and recovery from, mental illness, serious emotional disturbances, substance use disorders, and co-occurring illness or disorders; or

children.label: ii
children.text: integrating or coordinating physical health services and mental and substance use disorders services; and

children.label: i
children.text: applying such evidence-based programs to the delivery of care, including by training staff in effective evidence-based treatments; or

children.label: ii
children.text: integrating such evidence-based programs into models of care across specialties and jurisdictions.

children.heading: Consultation
children.label: 2 Consultation
children.text: In awarding grants under this subsection, the Assistant Secretary shall, as appropriate, consult with the Chief Medical Officer, appointed under , the advisory councils described in , the National Institute of Mental Health, the National Institute on Drug Abuse, and the National Institute on Alcohol Abuse and Alcoholism, as appropriate.section 290aa(g) of this titlesection 290aa–1 of this title

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: f Authorization of appropriations
children.text: To carry out this section, there is authorized to be appropriated $10,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2023 through 2027.
